inline_comments.message: In theory, you'll need the +x for other users other than apexuser to be able to do, "cd $POLICY_HOME/etc/ssl".   You may be OK provided that apexuser would own this directory, not sure, probably should test it out.   Traditionally the +x is needed to go into directories, etc .. (case ../etc/ssl), for regular files 644 is OK the change you made.

actually, could delete this line, as lines 45 & 46 do what's needed.  Might also be safer to move 45-47 to the end of this list so it applies to anything that happens to be copied into /opt/app by some prior step

Yes, apexuser owns this directory, so there shouldn't be any problem.
Jim I think you are right. But not sure whether we should make these changes at this point. I would like to test these changes, because earlier also 45-47 lines were there. But still had to add 49-50 to resolve some issues that came up during testing. The main change was making apexuser the owner of the directory and that should be enough(probably 45-46 have to be moved down as you said). Still I think it is better to spent some time to test these changes.

If you leave it as is right now, then Jorge's concern is still valid: may need to add:

  chmod 755 etc/ssl
